title: cmate-worktree-cleanup plan (version 1)
description: The dry-run preview a cleanup run produces before any deletion. Every candidate worktree discovered from git metadata appears here with its status, its merge proof, and whether apply would delete or skip it. A candidate is deletable only when proof.type is direct or merged_equivalent; everything else is skipped. Closed schema: an unknown field is a contract violation.

mode.description: A plan is always a dry run. Deletion happens only in a later apply run, recorded in cleanup-result.v1.json.

candidates.description: Every eligible worktree discovered from git worktree list --porcelain, after excluding the entries in `excluded`.

excluded.description: Worktrees that were never treated as candidates: the current worktree, integration worktrees, and worktrees outside the requested target set. These are never deletable, whatever the input asked for.

blocking_reasons.description: Why a plan could not be produced at all. Non-empty means no candidate is deletable.

limitations.description: Capabilities that were unavailable and the fallback taken. A fetch that failed belongs here as well as in `fetch`.

git_oid.description: A full 40-hex git object id. A commit id is not a secret and is recorded as proof; a raw GitHub API payload is never recorded.

git_ref.description: A branch or ref name such as feature/1449-cmate-worktree-cleanup or origin/main. Not an absolute path.

worktree_ref.description: A redacted, non-sensitive label for a worktree: its directory basename as git reports it (for example commandmate-issue-135). It carries no slash on purpose, so it can never hold an absolute path, a home directory, or a parent-escape. The live absolute path used to run git commands is never persisted here.

name.description: The profile in effect, for example commandmate or commandagent, or a user-supplied name. Nothing about branch, base, path or baseline is hardcoded; every value comes from the resolved profile.

verified.description: true only for profiles whose discovery and base resolution were confirmed against a real repository (Node/CommandMate and Rust/CommandAgent). An unverified profile is usable only after the user confirms it for this run.

base.description: The merge base ref every proof is measured against, resolved from the profile. Never assumed to be develop or main.

remote.description: The remote whose base is fetched for freshness, for example origin.

baseline.description: The ref tree equality is compared against when it differs from `base`, resolved from the profile. null when `base` is the baseline.

selection_mode.description: issues: only the listed issue numbers. all_eligible: every candidate the profile recognises, still excluding current and integration worktrees.

targets.description: Issue numbers when selection_mode is issues; empty when all_eligible. The skill never accepts a filesystem path as a target: worktrees are resolved from git metadata, not from a path the caller typed.

succeeded.description: false means remote freshness is unknown. Every candidate that needs the remote is then unverifiable; the plan never presents a stale base as current.

type.description: direct: the branch tip is an ancestor of base. merged_equivalent: not an ancestor, but all four squash/rebase conditions held. unverifiable: neither could be proven; the target is not deletable.

ancestor_verified.description: Result of git merge-base --is-ancestor <tip> <base>. Sole condition for type direct.

merged_pr_exact.description: Condition 1: exactly one merged PR whose head is this branch and whose base is the profile base. Zero or several is not exact.

head_oid_match.description: Condition 2: the PR headRefOid equals the worktree tip.

merge_commit_reachable.description: Condition 3: the merge commit is a real commit reachable from the freshly fetched base.

tree_equal.description: Condition 4: the net tree change the branch makes over its merge base is identical to the change the merge commit introduced into base.

unverifiable_reasons.description: Present and non-empty when type is unverifiable. Records every condition that failed, so the user sees why a merged-looking worktree was kept.

branch.description: null when the worktree is on a detached HEAD, which is itself a skip reason.

state.description: clean is the only state a deletion may proceed from. dirty, detached, locked and missing are all zero-delete.

decision.description: delete requires state clean and proof.type direct or merged_equivalent. Any other combination is skip.

delete_method.description: direct_branch_d for proof direct: git worktree remove then git branch -d. guarded_ref_delete for proof merged_equivalent: git worktree remove then git update-ref -d with the verified tip as the expected old id. null when decision is skip.

skip_reason.description: Required non-null when decision is skip; null when decision is delete.

diagnostics.description: Servers, processes, tmux sessions, DB files and logs observed near this worktree. Display only: this skill never stops or deletes them.

reason.description: current_worktree and integration_worktree are refused even when named explicitly.

observation.description: What was seen, with no absolute path, no PID that the user would act on blindly, and no secret. A hint for a manual next action, never an instruction this skill carries out.
